title: HAVE_LICHEN_LINK preprocessor flag vs config name inconsistency
description:
lichen_l2.c:28-35 defines HAVE_LICHEN_LINK based on CONFIG_LICHEN_LINK, then uses HAVE_LICHEN_LINK throughout. This adds indirection between Kconfig symbol and code without adding clarity.

What goes wrong as-is: Reader must trace HAVE_LICHEN_LINK back to understand what config controls it. The #define doesn't add value since it's a direct 1:1 mapping.

Rewrite: Use #if defined(CONFIG_LICHEN_LINK) directly at each usage site, or if the name 'HAVE_LICHEN_LINK' is preferred for readability, document why in the comment. Current comment just says 'Include LICHEN link layer if available' which doesn't explain the alias.
close_reason: Opinion: HAVE_LICHEN_LINK macro is standard C pattern for config abstraction
